result.summary: Braintree exposes a GraphQL payments API, a server-side gateway HTTP API documented through SDK/reference pages, and client-side authorization artifacts for its SDKs; authentication uses merchant gateway API keys, OAuth bearer tokens for delegated access, and client tokenization credentials.
result.description: Braintree is a payments platform from PayPal for accepting payments, storing payment methods, and managing transactions, disputes, reporting, and related commerce workflows. It supports direct merchant integrations as well as delegated partner integrations through OAuth and client SDK authorization.

gateway_api_keys.label: Gateway API keys (public key + private key) for a Braintree merchant account
gateway_api_keys.setup: Log in to the appropriate Braintree Control Panel for your environment — [sandbox](https://sandbox.braintreegateway.com/login) or [production](https://www.braintreegateway.com/login). Open the gear menu, choose **API**, and copy your `public key`, `private key`, and `merchant ID` from the API Keys section as described in [Important Gateway Credentials](https://developer.paypal.com/braintree/articles/control-panel/important-gateway-credentials). Keep the environment (`sandbox` or `production`) with the keys, since Braintree issues different credentials per environment.

oauth_access_token.label: Braintree OAuth access token for acting on behalf of merchant accounts
oauth_access_token.setup: Create or configure a Braintree OAuth application from the Braintree OAuth docs starting at [OAuth overview](https://developer.paypal.com/braintree/docs/guides/extend/oauth/overview). Implement the merchant-facing OAuth flow to obtain an access token for each connected merchant, then use that token as a bearer token in API calls. For in-person integrations, Braintree’s [API Authentication](https://developer.paypal.com/braintree/in-person/legacy/guides/api-authentication/) guide confirms this bearer-token option for third-party applications.

tokenization_key.label: Braintree tokenization key for client-side payment-method tokenization
tokenization_key.setup: Log in to the relevant Braintree Control Panel, open the gear menu, choose **API**, then go to the **Tokenization Keys** section. If no key exists, click **Generate New Tokenization Key** as described in [Important Gateway Credentials](https://developer.paypal.com/braintree/articles/control-panel/important-gateway-credentials). Tokenization keys are environment-specific and are used by Braintree client SDKs for reduced-privilege client-side authorization.

client_token.label: Braintree client token JWT generated by your server for client SDK initialization
client_token.setup: Generate a client token from your server using Braintree’s server-side API / SDK as documented in [Client Token: Generate](https://developer.paypal.com/braintree/docs/reference/request/client-token/generate/) and explained in [Client Token](https://developer.paypal.com/braintree/docs/guides/authorization/client-token). This token is short-lived and is typically minted per session/request by your backend after it authenticates to Braintree with gateway credentials.
